Article Title: EC announces bypolls for 3 assembly seats in Bihar, MP and Gujarat; voting on July 30
Article Text:
EC announces bypolls for 3 assembly seats in Bihar, MP and Gujarat; voting on July 30

NEW DELHI: The Election Commission on Thursday announced byelections to three assembly constituencies in Bihar, Madhya Pradesh, and Gujarat, with polling scheduled for July 30 and counting of votes on August 3.Of the three seats, two — Bankipur in Bihar and Manjalpur in Gujarat — were held by the BJP, while Datia in Madhya Pradesh was represented by the Congress.The Bankipur seat fell vacant after BJP leader Nitin Nabin resigned as an MLA following his election to the Rajya Sabha in April. Nabin was appointed the party's national president in January.The Manjalpur constituency fell vacant following the death of BJP MLA Yogeshbhai Narandas Patel in June.The Datia seat was vacated after Congress MLA Rajendra Bharti was disqualified following his conviction in a bank scam case.The Election Commission said the notification for the bypolls will be issued on July 6. The last date for filing nominations is July 13, scrutiny of nomination papers will be held on July 14, and candidates can withdraw their nominations until July 16.
Article Summary: EC announces bypolls for 3 assembly seats in Bihar, MP and Gujarat; voting on July 30. Of the three seats, two — Bankipur in Bihar and Manjalpur in Gujarat — were held by the BJP. Datia in Madhya Pradesh was represented by the Congress.

Article Title: BJP splitting parties, but won't get 2/3rd majority, says Congress
Article Text:
Jairam Ramesh

NEW DELHI: As the dates for the monsoon session were announced, Congress general secretary in charge of communications Jairam Ramesh slammed the BJP, accusing it of splitting parties. He claimed the govt would still fail to secure a two-thirds majority."We are going into this monsoon session knowing that they will bring back the delimitation bill. They might also bring 'One Nation, One Election'....," Ramesh said while speaking to ANI. While noting that the split in TMC and Shiv Sena (UBT) has been a worrisome development for the opposition, Ramesh asserted "but we will not let them have a two-thirds majority"."Their real motive is to change the Constitution of India... The purpose of crossing 400 seats was to be in a position to change the Constitution," he said, recalling the BJP's 'Ab ki Baar, 400 Paar' slogan in the 2024 Lok Sabha polls, in which the BJP fell short of the majority mark of 272 on its own."Despite many efforts, they didn't get a two-thirds majority... They're splitting parties. But they're not going to get a two-thirds majority... they will not succeed," Ramesh claimed.
Article Summary: Congress general secretary in charge of communications Jairam Ramesh slammed the BJP, accusing it of splitting parties. He claimed the govt would still fail to secure a two-thirds majority. While noting that the split in TMC and Shiv Sena (UBT) has been a worrisome development for the opposition.

Article Title: 'BJP taking over our offices across Bengal': Mamata faction's Saugata Roy after rebels 'lock' TMC's Kolkata premises
Article Text:
TMC MP Saugata Roy

NEW DELHI: Trinamool Congress (TMC) MP Saugata Roy on Saturday alleged that the BJP was attempting to take control of the party's offices across West Bengal, a day after TMC's rebel faction took possession of the Mamata Banerjee-led party's office in Kolkata.Reacting to the development, Roy dismissed rebel faction leader Ritabrata Banerjee's claim that the dissident group is the "real" TMC."Ritabrata was a CPM man. Why does he keep talking about the party? His words carry no weight," Roy told ANI. Ritabrata Banerjee joined the Trinamool Congress in 2020 after resigning from the CPM in 2017.Referring to the incident at the TMC's office, Roy alleged that a group of people had locked the premises and that the police stopped him from removing the lock."Yesterday, some people came and put a lock on our office. When I went to break the lock, the police arrived and wouldn't allow me to even touch it," he said.Claiming that the incident reflected a wider pattern, Roy added, "That is exactly what the BJP is doing. They are taking over our offices all across Bengal. They are attacking our grassroots workers. The situation in West Bengal is very bad."Roy asserted that the TMC would continue its political fight despite the recent developments."This is a long war, and we will fight. We don't have any other option left," he stated.On Friday, rebel Trinamool MLAs led by Ritabrata Banerjee, Firhad Hakim, Jawed Khan and Golam Rabbani took control of the party office on the ground floor of the Metropolitan Building off EM Bypass before locking the premises and leaving.This came even as the Election Commission has directed both TMC factions to submit their claims and counterclaims by July 6 over which group is the "real" TMC.
Article Summary: Trinamool Congress (TMC) MP Saugata Roy on Saturday alleged that the BJP was attempting to take control of the party's offices across West Bengal. Roy dismissed rebel faction leader Ritabrata Banerjee's claim that the dissident group is the "real" TMC.

Article Title: BJP approaches EC over alleged interference in Karnataka voter roll revision
Article Text:
A BJP delegation led by state president BY Vijayendra met the chief electoral officer on Saturday.

Poll Vote & Share your view Should the Election Commission intervene in the electoral roll revision process in Karnataka? Yes, they should intervene. No, it's not necessary. 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

NEW DELHI: The BJP has approached the Election Commission alleging that the Congress government in Karnataka is interfering with the ongoing revision of electoral rolls. The party claimed that officials responsible for updating voter lists are being prevented from carrying out their work in the prescribed manner and urged the poll body to intervene.A BJP delegation led by state president BY Vijayendra, along with senior MLA and former minister S Suresh Kumar, met the chief electoral officer on Saturday and submitted a formal complaint over the issue.Speaking to reporters after the meeting, Vijayendra alleged that the Election Commission had launched the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) of electoral rolls with the right intent, but the state government was attempting to disrupt the exercise.He claimed that instead of allowing Booth Level Officers (BLOs) to perform their duties independently, the government was putting them under pressure."Today, pressure is being exerted on Booth Level Officers (BLOs), and they are being misused," the Shikaripura MLA alleged.Vijayendra said the SIR exercise requires BLOs to visit households and verify voter details through a door-to-door process."However, the BLOs are being made to sit in community halls, marriage halls, in some places in mosques, and in some places even in the offices of MLAs. We have brought such instances to the notice of the Election Commission," Vijayendra said.The BJP has requested the Election Commission to examine the allegations and ensure that the electoral roll revision is carried out strictly in accordance with the prescribed rules and procedures.
Article Summary: The BJP has approached the Election Commission alleging that the Congress government in Karnataka is interfering with the ongoing revision of electoral rolls. The party claimed that officials responsible for updating voter lists are being prevented from carrying out their work in the prescribed manner. A BJP delegation led by state president BY Vijayendra met the chief electoral officer on Saturday.

Article Title: 'Transgression': BJP meets Tamil Nadu governor over 2 'private individuals' at CM Vijay's official meeting, seeks action
Article Text:
Tamil Nadu CM C Joseph Vijay (File photo)

(With PTI inputs)

NEW DELHI: The BJP's Tamil Nadu unit on Saturday approached governor Rajendra Arlekar, seeking action against two "private individuals" for allegedly attending a cabinet meeting chaired by chief minister C Joseph Vijay despite holding no official government position.State BJP chief Nainar Nagenthran identified the duo as John Arokiyasamy and Vishnu Reddy. They are the same men against whom the DMK has also lodged a police complaint , alleging that they attended official meetings and issued directions to government officials."I have petitioned governor Arlekar to take appropriate action," Nagenthran told reporters after meeting the governor along with Tamil Nadu BJP chief spokesperson Narayanan Thirupathy and state in-charge Arvind Menon.According to a Lok Bhavan release, the BJP's memorandum alleged that the two individuals attended the recently concluded Tamil Nadu cabinet meeting alongside ministers, calling it an "illegal act and a violation of the oath of secrecy."The BJP delegation "strongly condemned" the alleged transgression and urged the governor to initiate appropriate legal action, the release said.The memorandum also requested Governor Arlekar to advise Chief Minister Vijay to ensure that such incidents are not repeated.Meanwhile, the ruling TVK has maintained that both Arokiyasamy and Reddy have been appointed as special advisers to the chief minister and are therefore no longer private individuals.According to the DMK's police complaint, both men are residents of Andhra Pradesh.
Article Summary: The BJP's Tamil Nadu unit on Saturday approached governor Rajendra Arlekar, seeking action against two "private individuals" The duo are John Arokiyasamy and Vishnu Reddy. They are the same men against whom the DMK has also lodged a police complaint.

Article Text:
NAGPUR:





Sitabuldi police on Saturday morning have arrested Sanjay Petkar, a resident of Kamptee Road, who had been absconding for the last 24 years. He was later produced before the ad-hoc district judge.Petkar was wanted in a case registered under Section 353 of IPC at Sitabuldi police station for obstructing govt official from performing duty in 2002. He was accused of concealing his identity and repeatedly failing to appear before the court despite orders issued by the Ad-hoc District Judge.Acting on reliable information, a police team conducted surveillance and laid a trap after tracing the accused to Masepathar village in Kalmeshwar.
Article Summary: Sanjay Petkar, a resident of Kamptee Road, had been absconding for the last 24 years. Petkar was wanted in a case registered under Section 353 of IPC at Sitabuldi police station for obstructing govt official from performing duty in 2002.

Article Text: Chennai: A 24-year-old construction supervisor from Bihar was robbed of ₹3,000 in cash and his mobile phone after two unidentified men lured him into an auto-rickshaw on the pretext of helping him book a train ticket near Perambur railway station in Chennai on Friday.The suspects took him to a secluded area in Sembium, threatened him with a knife, robbed him and fled. The Thiru Vi Ka Nagar police registered a case.The victim, Ashok Kumar , 24, a native of Bihar, lived in Nungambakkam and worked as a construction supervisor in the city.Ashok Kumar arrived at Perambur railway station on Friday evening to travel to his hometown in Bihar. While he was waiting for his train, two unidentified men approached him, struck up a conversation and offered to help him book a train ticket. Trusting them, Ashok Kumar accompanied the men in an auto-rickshaw. The suspects diverted the vehicle to a secluded location in Sembium, where they robbed him of cash and his mobile phone at knife-point.On his complaint, the Thiru Vi Ka Nagar police registered a case, secured the CCTV footage in the vicinity and launched a manhunt to trace the suspects.
Article Summary: Two unidentified men lured Ashok Kumar into an auto-rickshaw on pretext of helping him book a train ticket. The suspects took him to a secluded area in Sembium, threatened him with a knife, robbed him and fled. The Thiru Vi Ka Nagar police registered a case, secured the CCTV footage in the vicinity and launched a manhunt to trace the suspects.

Article Title: Shopkeeper accused of sexually harassing minor consumes poison
Article Text: Chennai: A 41-year-old grocery shopkeeper, accused of sexually harassing a 15-year-old schoolgirl in Poonthandalam, near Kundrathur, consumed pesticide after learning that a police complaint had been filed against him. He was admitted to Tambaram Govt Hospital for treatment.Police said Karthikeyan , 41, was making inappropriate remarks and asking the girl for a kiss when she visited his shop over the past few days. On July 3, around 7pm, Karthikeyan behaved inappropriately with the girl, who informed her parents.On their complaint, the Thirumudivakkam station visited the scene and conducted inquiries. Following this, panicked Karthikeyan consumed pesticide. He was rescued and admitted to Tambaram Govt Hospital.
Article Summary: Police said Karthikeyan was making inappropriate remarks and asking the girl for a kiss when she visited his shop. On their complaint, the Thirumudivakkam station visited the scene and conducted inquiries. He was rescued and admitted to Tambaram Govt Hospital.

Article Text:
The contrabands were destroyed in the presence of city police commissioner N Kannan, and deputy commissioners of police G Karthikeyan and P Thangadurai.





COIMBATORE: More than 270 kg of drugs seized from peddlers in Coimbatore city were incinerated at the Kovai Bio-waste Management Facility at Orrattukuppai on Thursday.These contrabands, seized in 140 Narcotic Drugs and Psychotropic Substances (NDPS) cases, were incinerated after respective courts directed police to destroy them at a bio-waste treatment facility. They included 270 kg of ganja, 334 gram of methamphetamine, 1.2 kg of ganja chocolates, 39 mg of hashish and 5,168 painkiller tablets.The contrabands were destroyed in the presence of city police commissioner N Kannan, and deputy commissioners of police G Karthikeyan and P Thangadurai.
Article Summary: More than 270 kg of drugs seized from peddlers in Coimbatore city were incinerated at the Kovai Bio-waste Management Facility at Orrattukuppai on Thursday. The contrabands were seized in 140 Narcotic Drugs and Psychotropic Substances (NDPS) cases.

Article Title: Guest worker arrested for sexually assaulting minor girl in Coimbatore
Article Text:
Coimbatore:





A 50-year-old guest worker from Bihar was arrested on charges of sexually assaulting a 10-year-old girl with developmental disorders near Thudiyalur in the city on Wednesday.Police said the accused, is a relative of the survivor and resides near her house. The incident took place when the girl’s parents were not at home. The girl eventually informed her mother about her ordeal, following which a complaint was lodged at the All Women Police (Central) station.The accused was booked under Section 5(m) (penetrative sexual assault on child below 12 years) and 6 (punishment of aggravated penetrative sexual assault) of the Protection of Children from Sexual Assault (Pocso) Act. He was produced before the court and remanded in judicial custody.
Article Summary: Police said the accused, is a relative of the survivor and resides near her house. The incident took place when the girl’s parents were not at home. The girl eventually informed her mother about her ordeal, following which a complaint was lodged at the All Women Police (Central) station.

Article Title: Sengottaiyan flays previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies on Mekedatu dam issue
Article Text:
K A Sengottaiyan





ERODE: Tamil Nadu revenue minister K A Sengottaiyan on Saturday criticized the previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies to stop Karnataka’s move to build a dam across the Cauvery river in Mekedatu.Speaking at a consultation meeting of officials from different departments held at the Erode district collectorate, Sengottaiyan reiterated that chief minister C Joseph Vijay had urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to halt the move to build a dam in Mekedatu.During the review meeting, the minister told officials to deliver results quickly, saying the govt’s performance since coming to power warranted a thorough review and that the people’s demands should be met without delay. “Officers must complete necessary office procedures promptly and ensure citizens receive timely relief,” he told reporters after the meeting.The review, chaired by district collector S Kandasamy and also attended by handloom minister M Vijay Balaji, covered a wide range of administrative concerns.A major focus was the Athikadavu-Avinashi integrated drinking water project. Officials were directed to find out why more than 70 ponds were not getting filled and asked them to take remedial measures. Officials were instructed to begin silt-removal operations in the Bhavanisagar Dam to improve storage capacity and downstream supply.On allegations that liquor outlets were operating in violation of regulations, the revenue minister demanded concrete statistics and warned that strict action would follow where breaches were confirmed.
Article Summary: K A Sengottaiyan criticized the previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies to stop Karnataka’s move to build a dam across the Cauvery river in Mekedatu. He reiterated that chief minister C Joseph Vijay had urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to halt the move.

Article Title: Freed on bail, DMK MLA says TVK govt will fall in six months
Article Text:
DMK MLA ANITHA RADHAKRISHNAN WHO WAS ARRESTED WAS RELEASED ON BAIL BY MAGISTRATE

Tuticorin





: Shortly after being released on bail at midnight on Friday, former minister and DMK Tiruchendur MLA Anitha R Radhakrishnan claimed that the TVK govt led by chief minister C Joseph Vijay would collapse within six months.Speaking to reporters after his release, Radhakrishnan said, “We are not afraid of anyone. Standing by the DMK has always been our history. The dream of defeating us will never come true.”Radhakrishnan was arrested after the Madras high court dismissed his anticipatory bail plea in a case registered under charges of intentional insult to provoke a breach of peace and public mischief.After being questioned for more than five hours, he was produced before a judicial magistrate in Tuticorin shortly after midnight. His counsel sought bail on health grounds and submitted a medical fitness certificate. Accepting the plea, the magistrate granted him bail on the condition that he sign at Authoor police station daily for 10 days.Police said the case relates to a speech delivered by Radhakrishnan at a public meeting in Authoor near Tiruchendur on June 20. Anticipating arrest, he had moved the Madras high court seeking anticipatory bail, but the petition was dismissed on Friday.
Article Summary: DMK Tiruchendur MLA Anitha R Radhakrishnan was arrested after the Madras high court dismissed his anticipatory bail plea. He was arrested on charges of intentional insult to provoke a breach of peace and public mischief. After being questioned for more than five hours, he was produced before a judicial magistrate in Tuticorin.
